parsed_data.reason: Plan v2 completed: regime gate already integrated into measured baseline. Session weakness filter is WATCHLIST (improves max DD to 2.44% standalone but subsumed by regime gate in integration — identical metrics). Volume microstructure REJECTED (net negative). Need new orthogonal hypotheses: event-driven filtering, price-action confirmation, and volatility-of-volatility regime features.
parsed_data.plan_markdown:
## Research Plan v3: Event + Price-Action + Vol-of-Vol Orthogonal Exploration

| Stage | Name | Depends | Worker | Goal |
|-------|------|---------|--------|------|
| 0 | Session Filter Re-integration Check | — | any | Clean re-integration of cf_session_weakness_filter into regime-gated shell; confirm or deny if it adds value beyond regime gate alone |
| 1 | Funding-Rate Event Gate | — | qwen-1 | Build cf_* feature using funding-rate events to suppress signals during/around funding settlement windows |
| 2 | Breakout-Consolidation Pattern | — | qwen-2 | Build cf_* feature detecting consolidation→breakout patterns using ATR-based range compression/expansion |
| 3 | Vol-of-Vol Regime | — | any-free | Build cf_* feature using volatility-of-volatility (ADoA of ATR) to detect meta-regime shifts |
| 4 | Integration Round | [0,1,2,3] | any | Merge all PROMOTED features into single shell, full diagnostics |

**Verdict criteria**: PROMOTE if integrated backtest improves on ≥2 of (PnL, trades, max DD) vs baseline simultaneously with Sharpe ≥1.06. WATCHLIST if improves 1 metric without degrading others. REJECT if any key metric worsens.

tasks.theory: cf_session_weakness_filter showed standalone max DD improvement (2.44% vs 3.08%) but integration run produced identical metrics to regime-gated baseline — the regime gate may already subsume session effects. Need a clean integration test with full diagnostics to confirm whether it adds any marginal value. If truly subsumed, mark as NOT-PURSUED and free the hypothesis slot.

steps.instruction: Search research memory for cf_session_weakness_filter integration results to confirm current status
Clone the regime-gated strategy to create a test shell for session filter integration
Add cf_session_weakness_filter==1 as an additional entry gating condition (AND with existing cf_regime_vol_gate==1) for both long and short signals, then save as new version
Validate the combined signal logic before running
Run preflight readiness check
Execute full backtest with the combined session + regime gate filter
Run full diagnostics on the integration run

Compare run metrics to baseline (net_pnl=1520.78, sharpe=1.385, trades=388, max_dd=3.08%). If metrics are identical or worse, record as SUBSUMED. If any metric improves, record as WATCHLIST or PROMOTE.

tasks.theory: BTC funding rate events (8h settlements) create predictable short-term price distortions and increased volatility. A feature that identifies historically unprofitable windows around funding settlements could filter out low-quality signals during these periods. This is orthogonal to both the volatility regime gate (which filters by ATR percentile) and the session filter (which filters by hour-of-week). Event data is available via the events catalog and has not been explored.

steps.instruction: Search research memory for any prior funding-rate or event-based feature work
Check available event data in the catalog
Sync funding rate event data if not already synced
Preview aligned funding events with price data to understand temporal structure

Validate a custom feature cf_funding_event_gate that: (1) checks if current bar is within ±2 hours of a funding settlement, (2) computes rolling profitability of signals in those windows vs outside, (3) outputs 0 during historically unprofitable windows, 1 otherwise. Use the events alignment to mark settlement proximity.
Build feature dataset including the new cf_funding_event_gate column
Publish the validated feature
Clone the regime-gated strategy for funding event testing
Add cf_funding_event_gate==1 as entry condition for both long and short, save as new version
Validate signal logic
Run preflight check
Execute full backtest
Run diagnostics on the result
Run walk-forward evaluation for temporal robustness

Compare metrics to baseline. PROMOTE if ≥2 of (PnL, trades, max DD) improve with Sharpe ≥1.06. WATCHLIST if 1 improves without degradation. REJECT if any key metric worsens. Record in research memory.

tasks.theory: Markets alternate between consolidation (low range) and expansion (high range) phases. A feature that detects ATR-based range compression followed by expansion could identify higher-quality entry points — signals that fire during breakout phases (after consolidation) may have better follow-through than signals in already-expanded volatility. This is orthogonal to the regime gate (which filters by absolute volatility level) because it focuses on the transition/dynamics of volatility rather than its level.

steps.instruction: Search research memory for prior consolidation/breakout/range-compression feature work

args.query: breakout consolidation range compression ATR pattern

steps.instruction: Inspect available features to understand what ATR/range columns exist already

Validate a custom feature cf_breakout_quality that: (1) computes ATR(14) on 1h bars, (2) tracks ATR percentile rank over rolling 48-bar window, (3) identifies consolidation as ATR_rank < 25th percentile for ≥6 bars, (4) breakout = transition from consolidation to ATR_rank > 50th, (5) outputs: 1 during breakout phase (first 6 bars after consolidation exit), 0.5 during normal volatility, 0 during fatigue (ATR_rank > 90th percentile). This creates a quality gate for signal entry.
Build feature dataset including the new cf_breakout_quality column
Publish the validated feature
Clone the regime-gated strategy for breakout pattern testing
Add cf_breakout_quality>=0.5 as entry condition for both long and short signals, save as new version
Validate signal logic
Run preflight check
Execute full backtest
Run diagnostics on the result
Run walk-forward evaluation

Compare metrics to baseline. PROMOTE if ≥2 of (PnL, trades, max DD) improve with Sharpe ≥1.06. WATCHLIST if 1 improves without degradation. REJECT if any key metric worsens. Record in research memory.

tasks.theory: The current regime gate filters by ATR percentile level. But volatility itself has regimes — periods of stable low vol, stable high vol, and transitional (unstable) periods. A vol-of-vol feature (standard deviation of ATR changes, or ATR of ATR) could detect these meta-regimes. Suppressing signals during vol-of-vol spikes (unstable transitions) could reduce whipsaw losses. This is orthogonal because it measures the second derivative of volatility, not the level.

steps.instruction: Search research memory for prior vol-of-vol or volatility regime stability work

args.query: volatility of volatility meta regime stability ATR

steps.instruction: Validate a custom feature cf_vol_stability that: (1) computes ATR(14) on 1h bars, (2) computes rolling stddev of ATR changes over 24-bar window (vol-of-vol), (3) computes percentile rank of vol-of-vol over 120-bar lookback, (4) outputs 1 when vol-of-vol rank < 70 (stable volatility regime), 0 when rank >= 70 (volatile transitions). This filters out signals during unstable volatility transitions.
Build feature dataset including cf_vol_stability
Publish the validated feature
Clone the regime-gated strategy for vol-of-vol testing
Add cf_vol_stability==1 as entry condition for both long and short, save as new version
Validate signal logic
Run preflight check
Execute full backtest
Run diagnostics on the result
Run walk-forward evaluation

Compare metrics to baseline. PROMOTE if ≥2 of (PnL, trades, max DD) improve with Sharpe ≥1.06. WATCHLIST if 1 improves without degradation. REJECT if any key metric worsens. Record in research memory.

tasks.theory: Merge all PROMOTED features from stages 0-3 into a single strategy shell. Test whether orthogonal filtering layers compound benefits without excessive trade reduction or mutual cancellation. Run full diagnostics + walk-forward + condition stability.

steps.instruction: Search research memory for all PROMOTED and WATCHLIST features from stages 0-3
Clone the regime-gated strategy as integration base
Add all PROMOTED cf_* features as AND entry conditions. For WATCHLIST features, add them separately and test both with and without. Save as new version.
Validate the combined signal logic
Run preflight check
Execute full backtest
Run full diagnostics suite
Run walk-forward for temporal robustness
Run condition stability analysis
Run layer comparison analysis to check each feature's marginal contribution

Final verdict: PROMOTE only if integrated run improves ≥2 of (PnL, trades, max DD) vs baseline with Sharpe ≥1.06. Record comprehensive results in research memory. If PROMOTED, record as candidate for live deployment.

parsed_data.cumulative_summary:
## Research State after Plan v2

### Active Baseline
- active-signal-v1-regime-gate@2: sharpe=1.385, net_pnl=$1520.78, PF=1.494, WR=0.503, max_dd=3.08%, trades=388, calmar=4.94
- This includes cf_regime_vol_gate==1 entry filter (ATR-percentile volatility gating)

### PROMOTED
- **cf_regime_vol_gate**: Filters signals when ATR(14) is in bottom 30th percentile. Now integrated into baseline shell.

### WATCHLIST
- **cf_session_weakness_filter**: Hour-of-week rolling PF classifier, suppresses weak sessions (PF<0.9). Standalone: sharpe=1.056, PnL=$1308, max_dd=2.44%, trades=498. Integration attempt produced IDENTICAL metrics to regime-gated baseline — likely subsumed by regime gate. Stage 0 of v3 will do clean re-check.

### REJECTED / Anti-patterns
- **cf_volume_microstructure_signal**: Raw volume-imbalance with aggressive thresholding. Net negative (-$34), PF=0.92, only 41 trades. Anti-pattern: raw volume features with hard thresholds don't work — too sparse, no predictive power.

### Key Insights
1. Volatility regime filtering (level-based) is effective and now in baseline
2. Session-based filtering may be subsumed by volatility regime gate
3. Raw volume features are unreliable — need transformed/relative volume approaches
4. The baseline has room for improvement in max DD (3.08%) and trade count (388 vs original 498)
5. Sharpe 1.385 is strong — new features must not degrade it
principles: Seek orthogonal features (event-driven, volatility dynamics, price-action patterns) — not cosmetic rewrites of volatility level gating
Test standalone first, then in best research shell, then with best base strategy
Check cannibalization: what the new layer adds, removes, and whether net integration improves
Judge by SIMULTANEOUS progress in PnL, trade count, AND drawdown — all three
Reject ideas that fail full-history, walk-forward, or condition-stability checks
Volume features must use relative/normalized transforms, not raw absolute values
Session and time-based filters must demonstrate value BEYOND what volatility regime already captures
Record all results — negative results prevent repeated exploration of dead ends

This creates a quality gate for signal entry.

Validate a custom feature cf_vol_stability that: (1) computes ATR(14) on 1h bars, (2) computes rolling stddev of ATR changes over 24-bar window (vol-of-vol), (3) computes percentile rank of vol-of-vol over 120-bar lookback, (4) outputs 1 when vol-of-vol rank < 70 (stable volatility regime), 0 when rank >= 70 (volatile transitions). This filters out signals during unstable volatility transitions.
